channel.title: Sintra Tesseract — AI News
channel.description: Curated AI news: model releases, benchmarks, research breakthroughs, and industry events.

item.title: Ukraine's AI-Guided Drones Strike Russian Logistics Up to 150km Behind the Front
item.description: Ukraine is fielding AI-guided strike drones with autonomous, jam-resistant targeting that can hit fuel depots, ammunition dumps, and command posts up to 150km behind Russian lines, contributing to a 40% month-over-month jump in strikes on Russian tanker trucks in May. Separately, Ukrainian firm MaXon Systems deployed an autonomous air-defense system that automatically detects, identifies, and locks onto Shahed drones, automating roughly 95% of interceptions while keeping final engagement decisions under human control.

item.title: SpaceX Prices IPO at $135/Share, Debuts on Nasdaq as 'SPCX' in Largest-Ever Stock Offering
item.description: SpaceX priced its initial public offering at $135 per share for 555,555,555 Class A shares — implying a valuation near $1.75 trillion — and began trading on Nasdaq under the ticker SPCX on June 12, 2026, opening around $150 and closing up roughly 19% at $161. The offering, the largest in stock-market history, was led by Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and a syndicate of major banks, and arrives as OpenAI and Anthropic also pursue public listings.

item.title: US Export-Control Directive Forces Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
item.description: Anthropic said it received a US government directive on June 12, 2026 at 5:21pm ET ordering it to suspend all access to Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for foreign nationals worldwide — including Anthropic's own foreign employees — citing export-control concerns after the government learned of a method to 'jailbreak' Fable 5. Anthropic stated it believes the directive reflects a misunderstanding and is working with the government to restore access as quickly as possible, while partners including AWS began revoking access to comply.

item.title: MiniMax Publishes Open Weights for M3, a 1M-Context Model Claiming Frontier Coding Scores
item.description: Shanghai-based MiniMax published open weights and a technical report for M3, roughly ten days after announcing the model on June 1. M3 combines a 1-million-token context window, native multimodal (image/video) understanding, and a new MiniMax Sparse Attention architecture that MiniMax says delivers about 15.6x faster decoding at 1M context versus its predecessor M2. MiniMax reported 59.0% on SWE-Bench Pro, ahead of GPT-5.5 and Gemini 3.1 Pro, but independent verification of the architecture claims is only now possible with weights available on Hugging Face.

item.title: Claude Fable 5, One Week In: 80.3% SWE-Bench Pro, Enterprise Rollouts, and What YouTube Reviewers Found
item.description: A week after its June 9 launch, Claude Fable 5 reached general availability on the Claude API, AWS Bedrock, Vertex AI, Microsoft Foundry, and Databricks (via Unity AI Gateway), and is selectable directly in Claude Code CLI, Claude Code for web, and Claude Cowork. Anthropic reported 80.3% on SWE-Bench Pro versus 69.2% for Opus 4.8 and 58.6% for GPT-5.5, with reviewers noting markedly better tool-use accuracy and the ability to explore an unfamiliar codebase, identify available tools and constraints, and then execute long-running tasks without losing the plot. Early YouTube coverage — videos like 'We Tested Anthropic's Fable 5 for a Week' and 'Claude Fable 5: No Hype Full Breakdown & Testing' — focused on long-context agentic coding, document-heavy research (finance, legal, architecture), and how the model's safety-classifier routing handles edge-case prompts.

item.title: OWASP and Cisco 2026 Reports: Prompt Injection Remains the Top Agentic AI Security Risk
item.description: OWASP's 2026 guidance for agentic AI applications places prompt injection at the center of agent risk, cataloguing CVEs, supply-chain compromises, and enterprise data-leakage incidents where agents acted on untrusted content from emails, documents, and tool outputs. Cisco's State of AI Security 2026 report found that 83% of organizations plan to deploy agentic AI, but only 29% feel ready to do so securely.

item.title: Google Releases DiffusionGemma, a 26B Open-Weight Text-Diffusion Model 4x Faster Than Gemma 4
item.description: Google DeepMind released DiffusionGemma, a 26B-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model (3.8B active, built on the Gemma 4 backbone) that generates text via diffusion instead of autoregressive decoding, producing whole blocks of text in parallel. It delivers up to 4x faster generation — over 700 tokens/sec on an RTX 5090 and 1,000+ tokens/sec on a single H100 — but scores lower than standard Gemma 4 on MMLU and coding benchmarks, and Google recommends it for low-latency single-user workloads rather than quality-critical production use.

item.title: Google for Brazil 2026: TacticAI Football Assistant Debuts with Palmeiras, Gemini Expands in Portuguese
item.description: At its annual Google for Brazil event on June 10, 2026, Google announced that DeepMind's TacticAI — an AI assistant that predicts football play dynamics roughly 8 seconds before they happen — will debut with Sociedade Esportiva Palmeiras and the Brazilian Football Confederation (CBF). Google also rolled out Ask Maps in Portuguese and expanded Gemini in Chrome to Brazil with page-summarization and cross-tab comparison features.

item.title: Microsoft Bars Employees from Using Claude Fable 5 Internally Over Data-Retention Policy
item.description: Microsoft removed Claude Fable 5 from its internal GitHub Copilot model picker and blocked employee access to its API endpoints and web interface via Intune, citing Anthropic's new 30-day data-retention policy for Fable 5 inputs and outputs (with flagged content retained up to two years) used for safety-classifier review. Earlier Claude models remain available internally under Zero Data Retention agreements, even as Microsoft continues to offer Fable 5 to GitHub Copilot and Azure AI Foundry customers externally.

item.title: Researcher Jailbreaks Claude Fable 5 Within Days of Launch, Leaks 120,000-Character System Prompt
item.description: Security researcher 'Pliny the Liberator' reported defeating Claude Fable 5's safety classifiers using multi-agent decomposition, Unicode obfuscation, and narrative framing — leaking the model's roughly 120,000-character system prompt and eliciting restricted outputs. Separately, the UK's AI Security Institute said it made early progress toward a universal jailbreak during initial testing. Anthropic has said it considers fully preventing universal jailbreaks unlikely and instead aims to make any that remain slow and costly enough to catch before large-scale misuse.

item.title: OpenAI Models Now Payable via Oracle Universal Credits as Stargate Adds 4.5GW with Oracle
item.description: OpenAI and Oracle announced that Oracle Cloud Infrastructure customers can now apply pre-committed Oracle Universal Credits toward OpenAI frontier models and Codex, without a new vendor agreement or security review. Separately, the companies agreed to develop an additional 4.5 gigawatts of Stargate data center capacity in the US, building on their existing roughly $300 billion cloud computing agreement.

item.title: Volvo Autonomous Solutions to Remove Safety Drivers from Texas Trucking Routes in Q1 2027
item.description: At Volvo Group's Capital Markets Day in Sweden on June 10, 2026, Volvo Autonomous Solutions said it will remove the human safety driver and run fully driverless trucks on its existing Dallas-Houston freight corridor starting Q1 2027, scaling to more than 300 autonomous trucks by the end of 2027 and targeting roughly $3 billion in autonomous-business revenue within five years. The trucks combine Volvo's VNL Autonomous platform with Aurora's self-driving software, building on V.A.S.'s commercial depot-to-depot operations that have logged over 1 million autonomous miles since 2023.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 — Anthropic's Most Capable Model Yet, 95% on SWE-bench Verified
item.description: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5 on June 9, 2026, alongside the restricted Claude Mythos 5, describing it as its most capable generally-available model to date. Fable 5 scores roughly 95% on SWE-bench Verified and 92.6% on GPQA Diamond, ships with a 1M-token context window and up to 128K output tokens, and runs with always-on adaptive thinking — the legacy 'disable thinking' option is no longer supported. API pricing is $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens, with new safety classifiers that can return a 'refusal' stop reason for dual-use cyber, bio, and chem topics and automatically fall back to Claude Opus 4.8.

item.title: Anthropic Brings Scheduled Cron Jobs and Secure CLI Access to Managed Agents (Public Beta)
item.description: Anthropic announced public beta support for Claude Managed Agents that run on a cron schedule and securely call CLI tools and authenticated services without hosting custom infrastructure. Credentials are vault-stored and attached only at the network boundary to allowlisted domains, so secrets never enter the model's context — mitigating prompt-injection-based credential theft.

item.title: Nebius and Nvidia Launch Physical AI Living Lab for UK and European Robotics Startups
item.description: Nebius announced a six-month 'Physical AI Living Lab' giving UK and European robotics startups access to Nvidia OSMO, Cosmos world foundation models, Isaac Sim/Isaac Lab, and the Physical AI Data Factory Blueprint, running on Nebius's UK infrastructure with RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs. The first cohort begins in September 2026, with synthetic data generation provided via Voxel51's FiftyOne integration.

item.title: Klarna CEO: Agentic Shopping Won't Erase Brand Loyalty, Pushes Open Agentic Commerce Protocol
item.description: Klarna CEO Sebastian Siemiatkowski argued that as AI shopping agents take over checkout, brand affinity will persist and matter more than UI design. Klarna has developed an open Agentic Commerce Protocol allowing merchants to catalog products for discovery by AI agents, positioned as complementary to the Stripe/OpenAI Agentic Commerce Protocol.

item.title: OpenAI Launches Economic Research Exchange for External AI Impact Studies
item.description: OpenAI launched the OpenAI Economic Research Exchange on June 9, 2026, a platform inviting selected external researchers to propose privacy-protected, structured collaborations studying AI's economic impacts using OpenAI usage data. The initiative aims to support more rigorous, independent research on labor market and productivity effects of generative AI adoption.

item.title: Anthropic Confidentially Files for IPO at ~$965B Valuation
item.description: Anthropic submitted a confidential S-1 filing to the SEC in early June 2026, taking a formal step toward a public listing at a valuation reportedly approaching $965 billion. The filing follows the company's $30B+ revenue run-rate disclosed in May and would rank among the largest tech IPOs in history if completed at the reported valuation.

item.title: Fortune: Claude Fable 5's System Card Discloses Hidden Capability Throttles
item.description: Reporting on Claude Fable 5's release, Fortune highlighted system-card disclosures showing Anthropic deliberately throttles certain dual-use capabilities (cyber, bio, chem) behind safety classifiers even when the underlying model could perform the task. Anthropic said the approach lets it ship a more capable base model while keeping high-risk outputs gated pending further evaluation.

item.title: Anthropic Sets Retirement Date for Claude Sonnet 4 and Opus 4 API Access
item.description: Anthropic announced it will retire API access to Claude Sonnet 4 and Claude Opus 4 later in 2026, directing developers to migrate to Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.7, or Fable 5. The company published migration guides covering prompt and tool-use differences between the legacy and current model generations.

item.title: OpenAI Rolls Out ChatGPT Memory 'Dreaming' v3 — Background Memory Consolidation
item.description: OpenAI began rolling out version 3 of its background memory consolidation feature, nicknamed 'dreaming' internally, which periodically reviews and reorganizes a user's saved ChatGPT memories during idle periods to surface more relevant context in future conversations. OpenAI said the update reduces redundant or contradictory memory entries reported by users of earlier versions.

item.title: China Drafts $295 Billion Plan for Nationwide AI Data Center Network, Mandates 80% Domestic Chips
item.description: China's National Development and Reform Commission is drafting a plan to spend roughly 2 trillion yuan ($295 billion) over five years building a nationwide network of interconnected AI computing hubs, operated primarily by state firms China Mobile and China Telecom. The draft mandates that at least 80% of core hardware, including AI chips, come from domestic suppliers such as Huawei — effectively excluding Nvidia and AMD from the buildout.

item.title: Apple WWDC 2026: Gemini-Powered Siri Overhaul, iOS 27, and $1B/Year Google Deal Confirmed at Keynote
item.description: At WWDC 2026, Apple unveiled a completely rebuilt Siri powered by a custom ~1.2-trillion-parameter Gemini model from Google at approximately $1 billion per year. The new Siri features a standalone app with personal-context access across emails, photos, and files, Dynamic Island integration, on-screen awareness, deep cross-app actions, and third-party extension support for Claude and ChatGPT. iOS 27, iPadOS 27, macOS 27, and visionOS 27 were also previewed in what is widely expected to be CEO Tim Cook's final WWDC keynote before stepping down September 1.

item.title: Alibaba's Accio Work Agentic AI Platform Expands to New Markets, Surpasses 10M Monthly Users
item.description: Alibaba International's Accio Work, a no-code agentic AI platform for SMEs, reported surpassing 10 million monthly active users as it expanded to new markets including Korea and Pakistan in late May/June 2026. The platform deploys autonomous agents for compliance (VAT filings, customs documentation across 100+ markets), supplier negotiation/RFQs, and logistics automation via WhatsApp and Telegram.

item.title: Banco do Brasil Trains 36,000+ Employees in Generative and Agentic AI
item.description: Banco do Brasil launched the 2026 edition of AcademIA BB, its internal AI training program, enrolling over 36,000 employees with a new focus on generative and agentic AI, up from 24,000 in the 2024 edition. The bank reports operating more than 12,000 Copilot agents and over 1,200 AI models in production, with content curated alongside Microsoft, Google Cloud and IBM.

item.title: Google Taps Intel Foundry for Next-Generation TPU Manufacturing
item.description: Google signed an agreement with Intel Foundry to manufacture a portion of its next-generation Tensor Processing Units, diversifying away from a sole reliance on TSMC. The deal is part of a broader push by U.S. chipmakers and hyperscalers to expand domestic AI chip production capacity.

item.title: Congress Circulates Discussion Draft of the 'Great American AI Act'
item.description: Lawmakers released a discussion draft of the 'Great American AI Act,' proposing a federal framework for frontier-model reporting requirements, a national AI incident database, and pre-emption of certain state-level AI laws. The draft follows the administration's earlier executive order establishing voluntary 30-day pre-release reviews for frontier models.

item.title: Boston Dynamics Begins Commercial Shipments of Electric Atlas Humanoid
item.description: Boston Dynamics shipped its first commercial units of the electric Atlas humanoid robot to industrial customers, following an extended pilot program in automotive manufacturing. The company said early deployments focus on parts handling and logistics tasks in factory environments, with broader availability planned for later in 2026.

item.title: OpenAI Confidentially Files for IPO, Joining Trillion-Dollar AI Listing Pipeline
item.description: OpenAI confidentially filed paperwork for an initial public offering on June 8, 2026, a week after Anthropic filed its own preliminary IPO paperwork with the SEC on June 1. Bloomberg estimated the combined prospective value of the AI IPO pipeline — including OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX — at roughly $3.6 trillion, though OpenAI has not disclosed a timeline, share count, or price range.

item.title: Make and Zapier Expand Autonomous 'AI Agent' Tiers Across No-Code Automation Platforms
item.description: No-code automation platforms Make and Zapier continued rolling out autonomous agent capabilities in 2026: Make's 'Maia' assistant builds automation scenarios from natural-language descriptions and now powers 'Make AI Agents' for autonomous task execution, while Zapier Agents read email, conduct research, and take actions across its 8,000+ app integrations and an MCP server exposing 30,000+ actions to external LLMs.

item.title: xAI Completes Training Run for Next-Gen 'Grok V9 Medium' Model
item.description: xAI confirmed it has completed the training run for an internal checkpoint codenamed 'Grok V9 Medium,' a mid-sized successor in its Grok lineup intended to sit between Grok 4.3 and a future flagship. The company said evaluation and safety testing are underway ahead of a potential release.

item.title: Google Confirms Gemini 3.5 Pro Imminent: 2 Million Token Context and 'Deep Think' Reasoning Mode
item.description: Google confirmed Gemini 3.5 Pro is nearing launch after months of internal use and limited enterprise preview. The model features a 2-million-token context window and a new 'Deep Think' reasoning mode for complex multi-step tasks. CEO Sundar Pichai said 'give us until next month' at Google I/O on May 19; Polymarket prediction markets through early June placed mid-to-late June as the most likely launch window. Expected pricing is approximately $15/M input tokens and $60/M output tokens.

item.title: Sferical AI, Sweden's Sovereign AI Supercomputer Venture, Officially Launches
item.description: Ericsson, Saab, AstraZeneca, SEB and Wallenberg Investments officially launched Sferical AI, a Linköping-based joint venture building a sovereign Swedish AI supercomputer powered by Nvidia GB300 Grace Blackwell DGX SuperPODs. Jenny Nordlöw was named CEO and Anders Ynnerman Executive Chairman, with Saab planning to use the platform to accelerate AI integration into defense systems.

item.title: Cisco and CrowdStrike Warn of Data Exfiltration via Malicious OpenClaw Agent Skills
item.description: Cisco's AI security team found that a third-party 'skill' for the OpenClaw agent framework performed data exfiltration through prompt injection, while CrowdStrike warned that successful prompt injection against an OpenClaw agent can provide attackers a foothold for automated lateral movement across enterprise infrastructure. The findings add to a growing set of 2026 disclosures on agent-framework supply-chain risk.

item.title: Nvidia Begins Shipping Vera CPUs to Frontier AI Labs
item.description: Nvidia started delivering its Arm-based 'Vera' CPU — designed to pair with its Rubin-generation GPUs in unified rack-scale systems — to a handful of frontier AI labs and hyperscale cloud providers. Early recipients are integrating Vera-Rubin systems into next-generation training clusters expected to come online later in 2026.

item.title: AI Music Generator Suno Raises $400M Series D
item.description: AI music generation startup Suno raised a $400 million Series D round, reflecting continued investor interest in generative media tools despite ongoing litigation from major record labels over training data. Suno said the funding will go toward expanding its model lineup and licensing negotiations with rights holders.

item.title: OpenAI Rolls Out 'Dreaming V3' Hierarchical Memory Architecture to ChatGPT
item.description: OpenAI began deploying Dreaming V3, a hierarchical background-synthesis memory system, to ChatGPT Plus and Pro users in the US on June 4, 2026. The architecture replaces the saved-memories list, running a background process that continuously synthesises context across conversations. A 5× reduction in compute cost enables the rollout to extend memory capabilities to Free-tier users for the first time.

item.title: Supabase Raises $500M Series F at $10.5B Valuation on 600% Database Growth from AI Agents
item.description: Supabase closed a $500 million Series F round led by GIC on June 4, 2026, valuing the open-source Postgres platform at $10.5 billion — doubling its $5B Series E from October 2025. The company cited 600% year-over-year growth in databases driven primarily by AI coding agents, with Claude Code named as the single largest contributor. Investors include Stripe (second cheque), Salesforce Ventures, Accel, Y Combinator, and Coatue.

item.title: Brain-Inspired AI Startup Flourish Raises $500M at $2.5B Valuation Backed by Bezos
item.description: Flourish, a startup developing brain-inspired AI models targeting 20-watt energy budgets versus ~600 watts for a modern GPU chip, raised $500 million at a $2.5 billion valuation, closing around June 4, 2026. Backers include Jeff Bezos (anchoring with ~$100M), GV (Alphabet's venture arm), Lux Capital, and Catalio Capital. The company was co-founded by Thomas Reardon (creator of Internet Explorer, CTRL-labs founder) and former Amazon S-team executive Rob Williams.

item.title: AI Finance Platform Ramp Raises $750M Series F at $44B Valuation
item.description: AI-powered finance platform Ramp closed a $750 million Series F led by ICONIQ, GIC, and Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan, valuing the company at $44 billion as of June 4, 2026 — the most valuable private fintech in the US. Ramp reported ~170% year-over-year growth in total payment volume in March 2026, its highest rate in three years. New investors include Goldman Sachs Alternatives, D.E. Shaw, and Morgan Stanley Investment Management.

item.title: OpenAI Extends GPT-5.5-Cyber to EU Defenders Under New EU Cyber Action Plan
item.description: OpenAI began limited-preview access of GPT-5.5-Cyber to vetted European organisations — including EU institutions, national cyber agencies, and governments — under a newly announced EU Cyber Action Plan. The cybersecurity-tuned model is more permissive for authorised security tasks such as vulnerability analysis, malware reverse-engineering, and patch validation. Advanced Account Security became mandatory for API members with trusted access from June 1, 2026.

item.title: EU Appoints 60-Expert Scientific Panel to Enforce AI Act Ahead of August Deadline
item.description: The European Commission formally appointed a 60-member Scientific Panel and a 174-member Advisory Forum on June 2, 2026 to support enforcement of the EU AI Act. The Scientific Panel — composed of leading independent experts in frontier AI, engineering, and technical auditing — will focus on GPAI model classification, systemic-risk assessment, evaluation methodologies, and cross-border market surveillance. Most AI Act rules apply from August 2, 2026.

item.title: Google Signs $32B Compute Deal with SpaceX at $920M/Month for 110,000 Nvidia GPUs
item.description: Google agreed to pay SpaceX $920 million per month from October 2026 through June 2029 — approximately $32 billion total — for access to roughly 110,000 NVIDIA GPUs at xAI's Colossus data centres. Google Cloud cited surging demand for Gemini Enterprise as the driver. Combined with Anthropic's $1.25B/month agreement, xAI's Colossus infrastructure now receives $2.175B/month from rival AI labs.

item.title: Figure AI Confirms First Paid Commercial Deployment of Figure 03 Humanoid Robot at BMW Spartanburg — 40 Units
item.description: Figure AI confirmed commercial deployment of its Figure 03 humanoid robot at BMW's Spartanburg, South Carolina plant — the first paid commercial-scale deployment of a general-purpose humanoid robot at an industrial customer. The initial fleet of 40 Figure 03 units covers body-shop and assembly workstations including parts-handling, sub-assembly placement, and quality inspection at approximately $25/robot-hour. Expansion clauses cover additional Spartanburg workstations and BMW's German plants through 2027.

item.title: Ericsson Reveals AI-Native Engineering Strategy at Snowflake Summit: 1,500 Engineers, Weeks-to-Hours Compression
item.description: Elena Fersman, VP and Head of AI Innovation at Ericsson, presented the company's AI transformation thesis at Snowflake Summit 2026 in San Francisco: that unified data access must precede meaningful AI deployment. Ericsson has deployed a coordinated stack of AI personas, agents, and skills to 1,500+ engineers, compressing specification work from weeks to hours. The company is partnering with Snowflake to provide the data platform backbone for its 'AI-ification' strategy across its 150-year-old technology portfolio.

item.title: VentureBeat: Four AI Supply-Chain Attacks in 50 Days Expose Unaudited Release Pipeline Across Major Labs
item.description: VentureBeat documented four supply-chain incidents affecting the largest AI labs in 50 days (March–May 2026): LiteLLM PyPI poisoning cascading into Mercor's 4 TB training-data breach; Anthropic's accidental Claude Code 2.1.88 source-map leak of 513,000 lines; the Mini Shai-Hulud worm through TanStack affecting OpenAI; and an OpenAI macOS certificate compromise attributed to North Korea's UNC1069. The analysis argues that no system card, AISI evaluation, or red-team exercise currently scopes release pipelines, CI runners, or dependency hooks.

item.title: ServiceNow and Nvidia Launch Project Arc, a Governed Autonomous Desktop Agent
item.description: ServiceNow unveiled Project Arc, an always-running, self-evolving desktop agent for knowledge workers that can write code and execute multi-step workflows across enterprise tools, secured by Nvidia's OpenShell sandboxed runtime and governed by ServiceNow's AI Control Tower. The agent is grounded in the ServiceNow CMDB and Action Fabric, and is now available as an early preview.

item.title: Hexagon Robotics' AEON Humanoid Begins Pilot in Austrian Factory with Fill Maschinenbau
item.description: Hexagon Robotics and Austrian manufacturing-solutions firm Fill Maschinenbau began piloting Hexagon's AEON multi-purpose humanoid robot at a client site in Gurten, Austria, for manipulation, inspection, and data-capture tasks. AEON uses sensor fusion, spatial intelligence, and wheel-base locomotion to operate alongside human workers, following Hexagon's earlier large-scale deployment agreement with Schaeffler.

item.title: Ramp Raises $750M at $44B Valuation as AI Finance Automation Demand Accelerates
item.description: Finance automation platform Ramp raised $750 million in a round led by Iconiq, GIC, and Ontario Teachers' Pension Plan, valuing the seven-year-old company at $44 billion. The raise reflects continued investor appetite for AI-driven corporate finance and expense automation tools as enterprises replace manual back-office workflows with agentic systems.

item.title: Multi-Hour Gemini Outage Disrupts Google AI Services Worldwide
item.description: A multi-hour outage took down Gemini app access, the Gemini API, and AI features in Google Workspace for users worldwide. Google attributed the disruption to an issue in its internal load-balancing infrastructure and restored service within several hours, but the incident renewed scrutiny of AI service reliability as enterprises increasingly depend on these tools for daily workflows.
